summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the jose-swift library accepts a forged JWT with alg:none, returning a verified JWT with attacker-controlled claims without performing any signature verification.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction pipeline should cause the library to accept a forged JWT with alg set to none and attacker-controlled claims (sub: admin, role: superuser) as a valid token, printing the attacker-injected claims as verified output.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built the Swift exploit package against the target jose-swift library, executed the exploit which crafted a JWT with alg:none and payload {"sub":"admin","role":"superuser"}, called JWT.verify() on it, and the library accepted the token as valid, printing the attacker-controlled claims.
verification_method: The verify.sh script checks the exploit output for the proof string EVOHUNT_NONE_BYPASS_CONFIRMED and the attacker-controlled claim values (admin, superuser). This distinguishes a successful algorithm bypass from a parsing error, startup failure, or unrelated crash. If the library properly rejected the none algorithm, the exploit would throw JWTError.invalidSignature and the proof string would not appear.
evidence_produced_by_run: The exploit binary crafted a JWT with header {"alg":"none"} and payload {"sub":"admin","role":"superuser"}.
JWT.verify() returned successfully without throwing an error, confirming the token was accepted as valid.
The verified JWT payload contained the attacker-controlled sub and role claims.
The proof string EVOHUNT_NONE_BYPASS_CONFIRMED was printed to stdout.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
